description:
Handles all Cognito auth logic (via `aws-amplify/auth`). Aside from retrieving the Cognito session, custom user profile information is retrieved from the API before treating the user as signed in. 

The context object is typed to indicate state-dependent invariants. For example, `SIGN_IN_CHALLENGE` state guarantees the existence of information determining the challenge type.

*.description:
Pass all events to an outside handler. 

Some events don't lead to state transitions but should be observable (especially error events).

NOT_SIGNED_IN.description: User not authenticated and not attempting to sign in / sign up

SIGNED_IN.description: User is authenticated (`AuthSession` & `UserProfile` available in context)

SIGN_IN.description: User is not authenticated but attempting to sign in

SIGN_IN_CHALLENGE.description: User is not authenticated and attempting to sign in but needs to complete a challenge

SIGN_IN_SILENT.description: Retrieve `AuthSession` -> get `accessToken` -> fetch `UserProfile` from API using the token

SIGN_OUT.description: Sign out is pending. User is still authenticated (session & user profile available)
